title: Labor Day
note: Federal holiday and common family lunch visit day. Confirm guest meal availability with the dining hall.

title: Rosh Hashanah
timing: Begins at sundown on September 11 and ends at nightfall on September 13.
note: Jewish New Year. Good planning window for family visits around meals.

title: Yom Kippur
timing: Begins at sundown on September 20 and ends at nightfall on September 21.
note: Major Jewish observance. Some families may plan a pre-fast or break-fast visit.

title: Sukkot
timing: Begins at sundown on September 25 and ends at nightfall on October 2.
note: Weeklong Jewish harvest festival. Check whether any holiday meal or family dining arrangements are planned.

title: Shemini Atzeret & Simchat Torah
timing: Begins at sundown on October 2 and ends at nightfall on October 4.
note: Major Jewish holiday immediately following Sukkot.

title: Columbus Day
note: Federal holiday. Check dining hall guest meal policy before planning around lunch.

title: Veterans Day
note: Federal holiday and meaningful visit day for many families.

title: Thanksgiving Day
note: Major family dining holiday. Confirm guest reservations, meal times, and seating rules early.

title: Hanukkah
timing: Begins at sundown on December 4 and ends at nightfall on December 12.
note: Eight-day Jewish festival. Families may want to coordinate evening or weekend visits.

title: Christmas Day
note: Major family dining holiday. Confirm guest reservations, meal times, and seating rules early.

title: New Year's Day
note: Federal holiday. Good day to check dining room hours before planning a visit.

title: Martin Luther King Jr. Day
note: Federal holiday. Check dining hall guest meal policy before planning around lunch.

title: Washington's Birthday
note: Federal holiday. Often called Presidents Day by other calendars.

title: Purim
timing: Begins at sundown on March 22 and ends at nightfall on March 23.
note: Jewish holiday that may involve family gatherings and festive meals.

title: Easter Sunday
note: Major family dining holiday. Confirm guest reservations, meal times, and seating rules early.

title: Passover
timing: Begins at sundown on April 21 and ends at nightfall on April 29.
note: Major Jewish holiday. Families may want to coordinate seder or holiday meal plans.

title: Mother's Day
note: High-priority family dining day. Confirm guest reservations and meal times early.

title: Memorial Day
note: Federal holiday and common family lunch visit day.

title: Shavuot
timing: Begins at sundown on June 10 and ends at nightfall on June 12.
note: Major Jewish holiday. Check whether family meal plans need advance notice.

title: Juneteenth National Independence Day
note: Federal holiday. Confirm dining room hours and guest meal availability.

title: Father's Day
note: High-priority family dining day. Confirm guest reservations and meal times early.

title: Independence Day
note: Federal holiday and common family visit day. Check holiday meal schedule.

title: Labor Day
note: Federal holiday and common family lunch visit day.

title: Rosh Hashanah
timing: Begins at sundown on October 1 and ends at nightfall on October 3.
note: Jewish New Year. Good planning window for family visits around meals.

title: Yom Kippur
timing: Begins at sundown on October 10 and ends at nightfall on October 11.

title: Columbus Day
note: Federal holiday. Check dining hall guest meal policy before planning around lunch.

title: Sukkot
timing: Begins at sundown on October 15 and ends at nightfall on October 22.
note: Weeklong Jewish harvest festival. Check whether any holiday meal or family dining arrangements are planned.

title: Shemini Atzeret & Simchat Torah
timing: Begins at sundown on October 22 and ends at nightfall on October 24.
note: Major Jewish holiday immediately following Sukkot.

title: Veterans Day
note: Federal holiday and meaningful visit day for many families.

title: Thanksgiving Day
note: Major family dining holiday. Confirm guest reservations, meal times, and seating rules early.
